description: Protective-act narratives tagged by actor gender. Used for frequency analysis in claim 6.
methodology: Each passage describes a protective act; actor_gender is M/F/N (neuter/unknown). Mammalian data drawn from ethological literature.

narrative_corpus.text: The soldier threw himself on the grenade to protect his platoon from the blast.
The knight rode out to protect the village from the approaching army, his shield raised.
The bodyguard stepped in front of the president, absorbing the blow intended for another.
He positioned himself between the attacker and his family, arms spread wide, refusing to move.
The warrior king defended the city walls alone through the night, protecting the sleeping citizens.
The off-duty officer drew his weapon and protected the bystanders from the armed robbery.
The father stood at the door, protecting his children from the advancing storm waters.
The guardian of the temple turned away the invaders, protecting the sacred texts within.
The mother placed herself between her infant and the approaching predator, vocalizing loudly.
The woman shielded the child with her own body when the car mounted the pavement.
She hid the refugees in her home for three months, protecting them from the authorities at personal risk.
The grandmother stood firm at the threshold, refusing to let anyone past until the children were safe.
The elder woman positioned her body between the young ones and the fire, guiding them out first.
The nurse shielded the patients from flying glass during the explosion, sustaining injuries herself.
The organization acts as a protector of minority rights in the region.
The state fulfills its role as protector of citizens through enforcement of law.
He was known as the protector of the weak, a hero who answered every call for help.
The god appeared as a great protector, his arms encircling the entire nation.
The lioness circled the cubs, positioning herself as barrier between them and the hyenas.
She was the protector of the seeds — carrying knowledge of which varieties survived the last drought.
The caped protector descended from the rooftop, interposing himself between the mugger and the victim.
The regiment's colonel was called the Protector — a man who had never lost a soldier unnecessarily.
She wrapped her arms around the smaller child and ran, protecting him through the chaos.
The Lord is my protector and my shield; I shall not be afraid.
The volunteer fireman rushed in again, acting as protector for the family trapped on the second floor.

mammalian_protection_data.behavior: Mother defends cubs aggressively against adult males, wolves, and humans. Solo female protection without male present in >85% of documented cub-defense events.
Matriarch-led groups; elder females coordinate protective responses to predators and threats. Males live largely solitary. All calf protection is female-organized.
Post-reproductive females (grandmothers) lead pod navigation and predator avoidance. Protection of young is female-coordinated at all life stages.
Lionesses cooperate to protect cubs from infanticidal males and rival prides. Female-organized protection is constant; male protection is episodic and territory-focused.
Males patrol and defend territory; females protect individual offspring. Male protection is group-level and episodic; female protection is continuous and individual.
mammalian_protection_data.note: Male territory defense visible and dramatic; female infant protection continuous but less narratively salient — illustrates reporting bias

mammalian_protection_data.behavior: Dominant female is primary group organizer; helper females (often daughters) guard pups at burrow. Sentinel behavior distributed across sexes but pup-guarding female-biased.
Females form allomothering groups to protect calves. Males form coalitions for mating competition — protection of young is female-organized.
Both breeding pair defend territory and pups. Alpha female leads pup socialization and protection at den; alpha male patrols boundary. Roughly equivalent but differently expressed.

corpus_summary.note: Narrative corpus overrepresents male protective acts relative to mammalian base rate. This asymmetry is the claim.
